question: Why did PyPI introduce trusted publishers (OIDC) and recommend them over API tokens? What problems were they solving?
context: Eidos AGI uses trusted publishers for all PyPI packages. During setup of 7+ packages, the friction of registering pending publishers came up — each new package requires a manual form submission on PyPI plus environment creation on GitHub. We want to understand the security rationale so we can document it in foss-forge as a finding, and explain to future contributors why we chose this path despite the friction. We have a PyPI API token in our vault that would be simpler to use. We need to understand the tradeoffs well enough to make this a documented decision.
